message:
fix(storage): switch generic-search filter to escaped LIKE for consistency

The list-issues "trimmed" generic search clause was the only remaining
substring matcher still using the old `instr(lower(col), ?) > 0` pattern
after the rest of `list_issues_for_search`-style filters were converted
to `LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'`. That left two annoying inconsistencies in this
single SQL builder:

1. Mixed matching strategies — three other adjacent filters
   (title_contains, description_contains, id_contains, etc.) call
   `escape_like_pattern(...)` and emit `%pattern%` LIKE clauses, while
   this branch lowercased the needle and hand-rolled `instr` calls.
   The shape of the WHERE clause depended on which combination of
   filters the caller passed, which made the generated SQL harder to
   read in logs and harder to optimize.
2. No metacharacter escaping — `instr` treats its needle literally,
   but the rest of the codebase expects users to be able to search for
   strings containing `%`, `_`, and `\` without those being interpreted
   as wildcards. Once we standardize on LIKE everywhere, callers no
   longer get surprised by partial matches when their query happened
   to contain a wildcard character.

The replacement uses the existing `escape_like_pattern` helper (already
used four times above in the same function for the per-field
*_contains filters) and wraps the escaped needle in `%...%` so the
semantics stay "case-insensitive substring match" (SQLite's LIKE is
case-insensitive for ASCII by default, which matches what `lower(...)`
gave us before for ASCII text).

No call-site changes; the public filter shape is unchanged.
